free_text: A study of excitation functions for some residues
80-270 ug/cm2 was prepared by vacuum evaporation onto
100 cm3 detector of resolution 1.9 keV at
for about 9 hours. The average beam current of about
70 nA behind the target assembly was measured with
electron suppressed Faraday cup. The beam fluences
was measured to be 2300 uC in each irradiation.
Theoretical estimates of the cross-sections are
obtained using statistical model based code PACE-2
that uses the Monte-Carlo simulation technique
for the de-excitation of a compound nucleus. The
optical model parameters for the emitted particles
(neutrons, protons, and alphas), and the average
gamma transition strength are taken from the
The non-uniformity in the target material
Decrease in the oxygen ion beam intensity
